tokens: Okay, Jack, I'm going to make a very sharp right turn here, because I do not want to go down that hill (into the crater).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I could have gotten that dark mantle on the other side of that crater.
That's all it would have taken me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: So you saw a radial orange (soil distribution inside the crater wall), huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You could see it very...It'll be in the pictures.
Oh, man, I can't drive into that heading (because of sunlight reflection off the LCRU.) Let me get my...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That was on the inside of the crater?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: On the inside rim of the crater.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's where the surface stuff keeps slumping off as it's exposed, probably.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, I'll tell you, that heading is going to put us right...Okay, Bob, give me a...Dang.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The heading you should be generally taking...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, can you give me a bearing and range at Victory?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: They got everything (they wanted at) that station; but not everything I'd like to give them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Man, I tell you that LCRU is terrible when it flashes into you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's what I've got to do.
I've got to tack into that Sun.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We got to go to Victory.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, I didn't have time to really think at that station; but that could...I think, based on having seen the alter(ation)...If I hadn't seen that alteration and all I'd seen was the fractured block on the rim - which looked like the stuff (the blocks) in the bottom - I might have said it was just another impact (crater).
But having all the color changes and everything, I think we might have to consider that it could be a volcanic vent.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm not sure how we prove it.
We didn't have time to prove it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I guess that's the breaks of the game, sometime.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, Bob, I forgot your numbers at Victory.
How about giving them to me again?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And it'll be a heading of 090...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...that's the general heading in that direction.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I guess we always have Station 9 to look forward to, guys.
That may be the same thing...We'll probably be out of time when we get to that one, too.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, Bob, a note on those (battery) radiators: I have been dusting the covers at every stop, whether that's any help or not.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're still about on the...Well, I think we moved...Yeah, we moved out into the Tortilla Flat area, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Those kind (of shallow craters) I can go through...If I can see them coming.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, Victory is going to be subtle, I'll tell you.
(Pause) Bob, how long we been out?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, I recommend that, if we ever do this again, let me get off and pick the charge off when we want to deploy it.
It really adds to the fatigue of the hands.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Couldn't you just hook it onto your fingers?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And Charlie (Duke)'s got a big smile on his face here.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: There's Victory over there, I'll bet.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can't see over there, but...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...got too much Sun in my eyes.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's the right way to go.
That'll be about it, too, (on range and bearing).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, I don't think I've really seen the LM, except (garbled)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Garbled) big rock in front of you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Well, you can't tell much about the countryside going into the Sun, can you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That'll give you a whole different perspective.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's got to be Victory over there, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) We're still seeing the pit-bottom glass...(Starting again) The glass-lined, pit-bottomed craters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's geology-ese if I ever heard it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Took you a while (to come up with the acronym), didn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Central Standard Time) down here, guys.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's 11 o'clock up here, too, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, it's square all right...or at least one side of it is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, three sides of it are square.
That's by accident, looking at it.
So how do we get over here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's where I'm going to go.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) If Charlie is smiling because my hands are tired, why did he let me get the charge off?
(Assuming a slightly sarcastic tone) Fine backup crew we got.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You guys didn't really mean to say that, did you?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: 106, 3.2 (bearing and range to the SEP transmitter).
We're approaching the rim of Victory.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the LMP frame count is somewhere around seven...Well, eight-five, maybe.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's Victory; look at it go to the left and look at it go to the right.
That's Victory; we're right on the ridge.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...on a Rover pan, you guys...Copy that (bearing/range readout).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Tell me where you want that thing (the seismic charge) and we'll get a pan around it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You see right ahead of you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (It) looks like a place you could spin a profile on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going, right here; you could put it in that hole.
No, you don't want to do that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just pick a spot and take your photos.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Pull out (the charge antenna); pin 1 is pulled and safe.
Pin...Boy, these are stiff this time around.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Remember to push it all the way back in, Jack, and start from scratch.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I did; I did; I did.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: But now I can't get to the...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And look at the orange flag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's what you guys were sampling at Station 4, I bet.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, it's about that orange, only not quite as bright.
Okay, let me turn my (Rover power) switch on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Wait a minute, wait a minute.
Let me get that (charge) out a little more (away from the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, there's no question but that we're at Victory.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's the first crater that looked like I thought it would.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me change my (camera) setting here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, let's get a nice Rover pan here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, turn the other way first.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we'll get a Rover sample here before you guys leave, too, after the circular pan.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Look at the light mantle over there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You can sure see it now, can't you now?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Getting your (camera) settings changed fast enough?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let's get our Rover sample.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) And the Rover sample will be from the same locality.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, it's just a couple of meters from the charge, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I hope I didn't put too much soil in there for you (to be able to close the bag).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) Rover sampler works just as advertised.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And how about a frame count now, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You're jumping the gun occasionally but not very often.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Having trouble reading the frame count indicator) One hundred and six.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I guess we're ready to leave here, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, if they don't want us to stop here, I guess we leave.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're ready for you guys to leave there...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, there's nothing else here now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gene, can you swing out there and give me one look down north into Victory?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got to go by that way anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, you know, just swing around and point north so I can look in there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I never got a good look at it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, it's a series of three craters.
There's some boulders on the talus slope of the easternmost...eastern slope of the west...eastern slope of the southernmost crater, the one we're closest to.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, how does that look to you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know what it looks like.
The northwest end of the V has white...(correcting himself) light blocks on it - boulders - on the inner wall and right at the rim.
And the northeast end of the V looks like it has somewhat darker rocks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Part of that is shadow, but I think they are darker.
And they look like about the same as down here near the tip of the V.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Got to be careful on that one, because there's one sloping away and one sloping towards us.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; we are rolling, by the way.
And we're at 106 and...Well, we're still 3.1.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the rim itself, though, of Victory is not blocky.
There is some increase in fragment size, but that seems to be the result of some craters in the rim that have gotten below the debris that's covering it.
I'd say that Victory's somewhat like Horatio in that it has blocky inner walls but essentially a normal block population on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we've got a Rover sample going toward Station 5 (Camelot Crater) at about 103 and 2.5.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And that'll be just a grid (sic) sample.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: They're none of them just grid samples, Bob.
(Pause; Jack laughs as Gene turns to avoid a crater)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You see, you can't tell how deep they are until you get up to them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That one I could have gone through.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Look at the size of that one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's another one on the right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, that one doesn't have any fragments in the bottom of it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like someone walked across it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) I think that there's quite a variability in the thickness of the dark mantle in here.
Did you...I didn't notice us crossing that one tongue of light mantle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Right at Victory, but it didn't show up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Looking into the Sun, you can't tell any difference anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: However, I tell you, I certainly get the impression there is a mantle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know what it is, but the dark mantle exists.
(Pause) They're just...The craters...(Pause) These craters are just too big not to have thrown up blocks.
And they're either subdued by the mantle or they haven't penetrated it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And a lot of these blocks...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I think you probably have both.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'd say they've been subdued by the mantle.
That really imposes an impression on me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There are those that appear that way; like Horatio, for example, or the big ones.
But others, I think, are too young.
Particularly those that are big and have bright halos.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, yeah; but the only ones that look fresh and not (big) enough to penetrate are these little ones with the glass in then.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, there's been some big fresh ones.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now there's one with glass in it, probably.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And without any blocks on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that just has mostly the shock-indurated rock...instant rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're coming up to 103 at 2.6 now, so we need a sample up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me slowly go to the right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Right out in that little inter-crater area, right out in there is good.
If you let me guide you a little, I might get a rock fragment.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Move ahead about...Yeah, right...Just...No, that's good.
Good, good, good, good, good, good, whoa!
(Pause) Now we'll give it a try.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And that battery is still at about 132.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're allowed to go to 140, tonight.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I don't expect we'll make it (past 140).
I think we'll get done before that.
I'll tell you those batteries deserve any temperature they want today, after going up that...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And just press on the same heading you've been carrying there, Gene, and that will get you to Camelot.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We want the southwestern edge, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Do you want to go where Station 5 is, Bob (that is, to the planned spot on the southwest rim)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: So press on towards there unless I tell you otherwise.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, but you were talking about changing Station 5.
I think Station 5 is a pretty good spot (as is).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I think that's where we want to go.
I'm just trying to verify that.
You can go in that direction, though.
I'll get with you if it's not.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) It's probably the most concentrated boulder field on Camelot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You know where it is, and we think it's about 092 and 1.6.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: But you know where it is, so you'll find it when you get there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, we're going to run into something in a minute if it's...It (Horatio)'s probably right over that rim on the right, Jack.
Right off your right hand at 2 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You know, it doesn't have boulders on it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That should be it right over that rim.
You know, I see why Al (Shepard) and Ed (Mitchell) had trouble walking up Cone Crater (on Apollo 14).
You could stand right on the edge of the rim of a crater and not know it's there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Thinking back to Shorty) Man, that was spectacular.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) Could you see that color on the television?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, we couldn't see it, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) Look at the Sculptured Hills.
I'm sure glad I went up to take that second pan (at Shorty) to see that stuff go radially down into the center of the crater at that contact.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's there (whether the picture) comes out or not.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Look at...Look at up...up the (Wessex) Cleft over there.
You can see definite change in albedo now between the North Massif and the Sculptured Hills.
Well, you can't see it...Let me...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But, again, that may be your photometric effects.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, one's an upslope and one's a downslope.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just about right; but it's supposed to be darker in the cleft you know.
(Pause) I guess I've been on...(To Bob) LMP's back to Minimum.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I wish I had a movie picture of us driving.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That (movie) would be the classic of the century.
(Responding to Bob ) Well, there must be somebody out there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, the fragment population - we're at 099/2.0 - is still about the one-percent category of...And it's hard to tell, going into the Sun, what kind of blocks you're dealing with.
But my guess is - well, more than a guess - (is that) most of them look like they're slightly vesicular.
And, in that regard, resemble the gabbros.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a class of boulders that is flat topped and fairly-well rounded that is just about completely buried.
Only the...Not more than (about) 5 centimeters of it projects above the surface.
We've seen those off and on, both days.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And they seem to be quite distinct.
Now, whether it's just a continuation of the mantling, I don't know.
But most other boulders - the big ones - seem to project above the surface more than just that 5 or 10 centimeters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I tell you, the Sculptured Hills just have that wrinkled old-face feeling.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There are blocks over there though, aren't there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's blocks, but I don't see any concentrated outcrops...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...or concentrated masses of blocks up on the slope anywhere...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Do you think that's Camelot or not?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think that might be Camelot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...have blocks that far up the rim.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...Let me...Yeah, let me look in the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That kind of sounds like Camelot to us.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're coming right up at Station 5.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You going to park up on the rim so they can have a good (TV) panorama?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'd like to get a little on the other side of those blocks, if I can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Then they can look with the Sun on them (the rocks).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Because, otherwise, they can't see that other rim over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: So, I'll be all right, there.
I'll get to the other side.
Then they can look at these blocks and those across the way.
I got to go around this block field, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks just like it did before.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: So, we came right where we were supposed to.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All the blocks look very much the same in the wall of Horatio.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Didn't let any air out of that tire, did it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think my guess of 30 percent (block coverage of the surface) was reasonably good before.
Where are you going to park.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll park right over here, so that they can look in it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah; I got to head 045, so I head right into those blocks.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You still got to turn, remember?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, that's why I want to leave myself a little room over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
